$description: FieldOps Component Motion Tokens — per-component animation specs. Every token maps to a specific animated property on a specific component. A senior engineer can implement each component's animation by reading only its section. Format: {component}.motion.{trigger}.{property}: { duration, easing, from?, to? }

motion.$description: Buttons animate exactly one thing: the pressed/active state. Loading is the only 'long' state. No hover translate. No scale on tap (scale animations under a gloved hand feel laggy and imprecise).

bg.$description: Background color shift on :active. 100ms, no drama.

appear.$description: Focus ring MUST appear instantly. Zero delay, zero easing. Accessibility — any latency on focus indicators is a WCAG 2.4.11 risk.

spinner.$description: Spinner fades in (opacity 0→1) + label fades out simultaneously. 200ms. Cross-fade, not sequential.

rotation.$description: Spinner rotation: 360deg continuous. 800ms per revolution. linear easing only. CSS: animation: spin 800ms linear infinite.

spinner.$description: Spinner fades out fast. 150ms. Shorter than enter — operator expects label back quickly.

do-not: translate-y on hover (no nudge/lift — not a consumer app)
scale on press (imprecise under gloves, conflicts with 48px tap target integrity)
box-shadow bloom on hover (performance hit on mid-range Android)
color shimmer / gradient animation on loading

motion.$description: Input animations serve two masters: legibility (label must always be readable) and state communication (error is urgent but not alarming). No decorative motion.

border-color.$description: Border transitions from default to focus color. 200ms.

ring-appear.$description: Focus ring: instant. See Button.focus-ring rationale.

transform.$description: Floating label: translateY(0) + scale(1) → translateY(-20px) + scale(0.75). 200ms spring-gentle. The scale must be done via transform, not font-size change — font-size animation causes layout reflow.

color.$description: Label color shifts simultaneously with transform. Single transition block.

helper-text.$description: Error helper text: height 0→auto + opacity 0→1. 250ms. height animation: use max-height trick (max-height: 0 → 60px). Do not use clip-path — perf.

border-color.$description: Border snaps to error color faster than the text appears. 150ms sharp. Communicates: error detected, detail incoming.

shake.$description: DO NOT shake inputs on error. Field operators are filling forms under task pressure — a shaking input is disorienting, not helpful. Color change is sufficient.

helper-text.$description: Helper text collapses on valid input. 150ms. Faster exit = less distraction while typing.

do-not: shake/vibrate animation on error
background color flash on focus
placeholder fade (placeholder should simply disappear on type — no transition)

motion.$description: StatusBadge can be mounted (appears on page load or list update) or swapped (state changes in-place). These are different interactions with different motion contracts.

scale.$description: Badge scales from 0.7 → 1.0 on first mount. 100ms bounce-subtle. Only on mount, never on hover.

opacity.$description: Paired with scale. opacity 0 → 1. Same 100ms duration, same enter easing.

strategy.$description: When badge state changes (e.g. new → active → completed): cross-dissolve. Old badge opacity 1→0 (75ms exit), new badge opacity 0→1 (150ms enter). Color change happens at the 0-opacity midpoint so there is no visible color blend.

pulse.$description: The sync-pending dot on OrderCard pulses to communicate 'this data is not yet confirmed'. Pulse ONLY when in sync-pending state. Stop immediately when synced.

do-not: animate color directly (always cross-dissolve to avoid perceiving intermediate hues)
bounce the badge on every list refresh
pulse more than one badge at a time in the same viewport

motion.$description: AlertBanner mounts at the top of a view. It must be noticed but not startling. The most common trigger is an offline→error transition — operators must see this change without losing their place in a form.

transform.$description: Slides down from translateY(-100%) to translateY(0). 300ms decelerate. Does NOT push content down — it overlaps (position: sticky top-0) to avoid layout shifts mid-form.

opacity.$description: Opacity 0→1 over 200ms. Starts simultaneously with transform. The opacity finishes slightly before the slide — this makes the text legible before motion stops.

transform.$description: Slides back up to translateY(-100%). 200ms. Exits faster than it enters — once the error is resolved, operators don't need a goodbye.

opacity.$description: Opacity 1→0 over 150ms. Slightly faster than the slide — text disappears before the banner fully retracts.

error-mount.$description: Error icon snaps in sharp. No spring — this is an alert, not a delight.

success-mount.$description: Success icon uses spring-snappy. One micro-overshoot communicates 'done'.

do-not: bounce or shake the banner to attract attention — color + position change is sufficient
animate banners that are already visible (update text in-place without re-animating)
stack multiple banners with staggered animation — show the highest-severity only

motion.$description: OrderCard is the primary content unit in the field list. Two contexts: list (many cards, scroll performance critical) and detail-expand (single card expands in-place). Motion budget is tight here — every ms of animation is ms the GPU is busy while the operator scrolls.

shadow.$description: Box shadow transitions from shadow.sm to shadow.md. 150ms. GPU-composited (use filter:drop-shadow or will-change:box-shadow sparingly — test on Android).

border-color.$description: Border transitions to action.primary. 150ms simultaneous with shadow.

transform.$description: NO translateY lift on hover. List performance — 20+ cards translating simultaneously tanks frame rate on the target device (Android mid-range).

opacity.$description: Card dims to 0.92 opacity on :active. 100ms. Conveys tap without scale.

sync-pending-indicator.$description: The sync-pending corner dot uses the same pulse spec as status-badge offline pulse.

strategy.$description: When status changes on a card that is already visible (e.g. websocket push updates status from 'active' to 'completed'): the StatusBadge inside the card cross-dissolves per its own spec. The card itself does not animate.

priority-flash.$description: ONE-TIME flash when a card is promoted to high priority via push. Runs once, does not loop. Draws attention to a card the operator may have already scanned past. Implementation: add class .priority-flash, remove after animationend.

transform.$description: Cards reorder on sort change. Each card translates to its new position. 400ms spring-gentle. Use FLIP technique (First Last Invert Play) — do not animate top/left. Only translateX/Y.

stagger-delay.$description: 20ms stagger between cards in the reorder sequence. Maximum stagger applied to first 8 visible cards only — beyond that, all remaining cards animate simultaneously to avoid a domino effect that takes 160ms+ to complete.

do-not: animate card width or height in the list
use box-shadow animation on scroll (causes continuous GPU work)
apply entrance animation on every list refresh — only animate newly-added cards

motion.$description: GuidedStep orchestrates the core field task flow. Motion here is a communication protocol: blocked → unlocked → completed is a semantic sequence that must be visually unambiguous. The step indicator (circle) is the primary animated object.

unlock.$description: Transition: completion-blocked → completion-unlocked. Field data has been entered.

indicator-bg.$description: Circle background transitions from white to sage/500. 200ms.

advance-btn.$description: Advance button: disabled opacity 0.4 → 1.0. 150ms. Does NOT slide in — it was always there, just disabled. Opacity transition only.

block.$description: Transition: completion-unlocked → completion-blocked. Required field cleared.

indicator-bg.$description: Circle transitions from sage fill to error border. Sharp 150ms — communicates 'you removed something important'.

complete.$description: Step marked complete. The most celebratory motion in the system — one micro-spring on the checkmark.

checkmark-scale.$description: Checkmark SVG path draws in AND scales: scale 0.6→1.0 with spring-snappy overshoot. 200ms. The overshoot lands at ~8% then settles. Do not use stroke-dashoffset draw animation — too slow and conflicts with spring timing.

connector.$description: Vertical connector below the step fills from top to bottom (scaleY 0→1, transform-origin: top). 250ms. Runs AFTER checkmark settles (150ms delay).

step-bg.$description: Step background transitions to sage/50. 200ms. Simultaneous with checkmark.

next-step-enter.$description: The next step reveals (if it was hidden). opacity 0→1 + translateY(8px→0). 300ms. 100ms delay after connector finishes. Total sequence: 0ms checkmark, 150ms connector, 400ms next-step.

do-not: animate the step number/label text — it is navigation reference, not a state indicator
run complete sequence backwards on undo — instead: instant state reset, then re-enter from unlock
stagger multiple completed steps on page load — render them all as completed, no animation on hydration

motion.$description: OfflineIndicator is a persistent system tray element. Three states with distinct motion signatures — each state has a unique behavioral animation so operators know connectivity status from peripheral vision.

all.$description: All state transitions use sharp 200ms. Not spring — connectivity is infrastructure, not UI delight.

label-swap.$description: Text label cross-dissolves: old opacity 0, new opacity 1. 100ms each. Labels are short (max 2 words) — no layout shift.

icon-animation.$description: Offline icon is static. No pulse, no blink. The static nature communicates stasis — the system is waiting, not working.

icon-rotation.$description: Sync icon rotates. 1200ms per revolution — slightly slower than a loading spinner (800ms) to communicate steady upload, not urgency. CSS: animation: spin 1200ms linear infinite.

pause-when.$value: syncing complete — freeze rotation on the exact frame where icon is upright (0deg) to avoid a jarring stop mid-spin. Implementation: on sync-complete, add .freeze class that sets animation-play-state:paused then transitions away.

icon-animation.$description: Icon fades in/out (breath pattern). Slower than syncing spin — communicates 'waiting for network', not 'uploading data'. Different rhythm from syncing so they're distinguishable at a glance.

banner-enter.$description: If OfflineIndicator manifests as a top banner (mobile), it uses AlertBanner.enter spec. Shared implementation.

do-not: blink the offline indicator (blink is deprecated accessibility-wise and seizure risk)
play a sound on state change (operators may be in noisy environments or meetings)
dismiss the indicator automatically — it must be manually dismissed or state-driven

motion.$description: SignatureCanvas is a capture instrument, not a navigation element. Its motion design is almost entirely suppressed — the drawn line itself is the 'animation'. The only animated moments are: canvas ready-state entry, and post-capture confirmation.

opacity.$description: Canvas fades in. 200ms. No translate — canvas at mount position is its final position. The operator is about to draw; spatial jump would misalign their hand.

canvas-wipe.$description: Canvas clear is instant. Wipe animations (swipe-to-erase etc.) are actively harmful here — the operator needs to know the canvas is clean NOW, not in 300ms. Use a single synchronous ctx.clearRect() call.

capture-confirm.$description: After a signature is accepted (data URL captured, timestamp recorded). One moment of calm confirmation.

checkmark-enter.$description: A checkmark fades in over the completed canvas. 200ms spring-snappy. Same as GuidedStep.complete.checkmark-scale. Signals capture success.

canvas-dim.$description: Canvas dims to 60% opacity to indicate it is now read-only (captured). 150ms. The signature is still visible — opacity reduction, not color change.

panel-slide.$description: If the canvas panel slides up for one-handed 6-inch reach (bottom-sheet pattern): translateY(full→0) 300ms spring-gentle. Same as AlertBanner but from bottom.

do-not: animate the stroke rendering (hardware canvas API handles this — no CSS motion on the drawing layer)
add a 'pen lift' animation between strokes
animate the bounding box / capture rectangle
play any animation while the user is actively drawing
